title: Wavelength
short: The distance between successive crests of a wave, λ, measured in metres (m).
full:
Wavelength λ is the spatial period of a wave — the distance over which its shape repeats, from one crest to the next. It connects to frequency through the wave speed, v = λ·f, so for a fixed speed a longer wavelength means a lower frequency.

Visible light spans roughly 400 nm (violet) to 700 nm (red); radio waves are metres long; gamma rays are smaller than an atom.

The SI unit is the metre (m); light wavelengths are usually quoted in nanometres (nm).
